cards.front: What is an interface in Java?
cards.back: An interface is a abstract class that cannot be instantiated and contains only abstract methods and constants.
Default interface methods are methods declared in an interface with a default implementation, which can be used by classes implementing the interface.

cards.front: Can static methods be used in an interface?
cards.back: Yes, static methods can be used in an interface, however they cannot be overridden by classes implementing the interface.

cards.front: Can private interface methods be accessed?
cards.back: No, private interface methods cannot be accessed from outside the interface, not even from classes implementing it.

cards.front: What is the purpose of private interface methods?
cards.back: Private interface methods are used internally by the interface or its implementation, and are not intended to be accessed or used by other classes, including those implementing the interface.
